title: Refrigeration fouling reduction
abstract: Processes and systems for reducing fouling in refrigeration systems. The process can include compressing a refrigerant to produce a compressed refrigerant and condensing at least a portion of the compressed refrigerant to produce a first liquid phase refrigerant. The process can also include reducing a pressure of the first liquid phase refrigerant to produce a second liquid phase refrigerant and separating at least a portion of any vapor phase refrigerant from the second liquid phase refrigerant to produce an exchanger duty liquid phase refrigerant. A liquid solvent can be combined with the exchanger duty liquid phase refrigerant to produce a solvent modified liquid phase refrigerant. Heat can be indirectly exchanged from a process fluid to the solvent modified liquid phase refrigerant and at least a portion of a contaminant can be dissolved in the solvent to produce a cooled process fluid and a heated contaminant-rich solvent modified refrigerant.
